Modelling & Simulation of Wastewater Processes
SUMO Simulation of Denitrification and Bio-P
Using Internally Stored Carbon in a Sequencing Batch Reactor
Yizhou Pan
April, 2025
Supervisor: Dr. Martha Dagnew
Background

Sequencing Batch Reactors (SBRs) achieve biological nutrient removal by alternating anaerobic, aerobic, and anoxic phases. Polyphosphate-accumulating organisms (PAOs) store carbon as polyhydroxyalkanoates (PHAs) under anaerobic conditions, using it for phosphorus uptake and denitrification driven by internally stored carbon (ISC).

Glycogen-accumulating organisms (GAOs) compete with PAOs but do not contribute to nitrogen or phosphorus removal. Understanding their relative dynamics is critical for process optimization.

Research Objectives
  • Understand the mechanism of internally stored carbon in denitrification
  • Determine whether SUMO can model internally stored carbon dynamics
  • Quantify the contribution of stored carbon to denitrification and Bio-P
  • Evaluate operational conditions (DO, phase length) on ISC-driven denitrification
SBR Process Design
Fig. 1 — SBR treatment cycle flowchart
Anaerobic
2 h
Poly-P release · PHA storage · VFA uptake
Aerobic
3 h
Nitrification · Bio-P uptake · PHA consumption
Anoxic
2 h
ISC-driven denitrification · NO₃⁻ → N₂

12 L lab-scale reactor · 15 °C · 8-h cycle · SRT = 10 d · Raw wastewater from Greenway WWTP, London ON

SUMO Model Setup
Fig. 2 — SBR process configured in SUMO software
1
Trial 1: Default setup — nitrification OK but Bio-P and denitrification failed; PHA ≈ 0
2
Trial 2: Added sludge wastage (SRT = 10 d), DO setpoint → 0, VFA fraction ↑ 13.9% → 20%, CASTO ↑ 500 → 1500 mg COD/L
3
Trial 3: PAO VFA uptake rate ↑ 7 → 8 d⁻¹; GAO rate ↓ 4 → 3 d⁻¹ → model calibrated
Model Validation
Feb 2025
One-month validation — observed effluent data within RMSE bounds for all parameters
Mar 26
One-cycle validation — DO, sCOD, NH₄⁺-N, NOₓ-N, PO₄³⁻-P all within prediction bounds
Fig. 3 — One-month validation: sCOD, NH₄⁺-N, NOₓ-N, PO₄³⁻-P (Feb 2025; shaded = RMSE bounds)
Simulation A — Effect of Phase Length

Five simulations varied the anaerobic / aerobic / anoxic durations. Longer anaerobic phase promotes ISC generation; longer aerobic phase favors nitrification.

Sim. Ana (h) Aer (h) Anox (h) NH₄-N mg/L NOₓ-N mg/L Nitrif. Eff. Denitrif. Eff.
1232 0.6113 96.2%59.7%
2 ★322 5.310 71.3%63.9%
332.51.5 3.911 78.0%62.1%
4331 0.9114 94.4%56.0%
5412 153.1 35.6%84.3%

★ Sim. 2 selected as optimal balance for ISC-driven denitrification

Fig. 4 — PHA storage across simulations: longer anaerobic phase → greater PHA accumulation within each cycle
Simulation B — Effect of Aerobic DO

Six DO setpoints (0.5–5 mg O₂/L) tested under Sim. 2 operational conditions. Lower DO favors denitrification; higher DO improves nitrification. DO = 3–4 mg/L gives balanced performance.

DO (mg/L) NH₄-N mg/L NOₓ-N mg/L Nitrif. Eff. Denitrif. Eff.
0.5133.241.7%85.5%
1.0125.645.0%74.0%
2.098.455.7%65.0%
3.0 ★7.79.560.8%62.3%
4.0 ★7.01063.7%61.4%
Simulation C — Role of Bio-P in ISC Denitrification

Varying initial CASTO concentration (200–1500 mg COD/L) tested whether active PAOs are a prerequisite for ISC-driven denitrification.

CASTO (mg COD/L) PO₄-P mg/L Nitrif. Eff. Denitrif. Eff.
1500 (high PAO)0.07460.8%62.3%
10000.07579.5%58.9%
5000.9183.7%63.5%
200 (low PAO)8.084.3%60.2%

Finding: Denitrification efficiency does not significantly decline at low CASTO — Bio-P is not required for ISC denitrification, though active PAOs remain the primary PHA source.

Conclusions & Limitations
✓ SUMO Capability
SUMO can model ISC-driven denitrification after careful calibration of PAO/GAO kinetics and initial biomass
✓ Anaerobic Phase
Extending anaerobic duration promotes PHA generation, directly enhancing denitrification efficiency
✓ Bio-P Role
Bio-P is not a prerequisite for ISC denitrification; flexible process designs are possible without it
⚠ Limitation
Glycogen storage not modeled; PAO and GAO cannot be tuned independently in SUMO
⚠ Future Work
Integrate detailed microbial kinetics; measure PHA, VFA experimentally for improved calibration
✓ Optimal Ops
3 h anaerobic + DO = 3 mg/L aerobic yields best balance of nitrification (60.8%) and denitrification (62.3%)
Greenway WWTP, London ON · Lab-scale SBR · SUMO Simulation Platform Yizhou Pan · 2025 · Western University
